residual_risks: some source materials come from a public-oriented vault snapshot rather than from a standalone published repository
JSON Schema files are public projections aligned to live validator behavior, but the validator remains the stronger source of truth for edge-case semantics
API response examples are illustrative contract samples, not recorded live HTTP captures from a deployed public service
validator API envelope schemas are public projections aligned to OpenAPI and curated examples, but the live validator remains the stronger source of truth for runtime-only behavior
client recipes assume a compatible deployed validator base URL and bearer-token auth model rather than representing a guaranteed hosted endpoint
local hidden Codex state is excluded via .gitignore and must remain outside any public commit
community intake is public and explicit, but maintainer responsiveness and future issue volume still depend on actual GitHub operation after publication
PUBLIC_PROJECT_PROFILE.json is a concise reviewer/program summary and can lag behind the stronger source surfaces if maintainers stop updating it together with release evidence
PUBLIC_CAPABILITY_MATRIX.json is a high-level usability summary and can drift if maintainers add or remove strong public surfaces without updating the matrix
PUBLIC_BOUNDARY_MAP.json is a curated summary of published vs deferred boundaries and can drift if scope docs change without updating the boundary map
PUBLIC_PORTABILITY_PROFILE.json and the archive-bundle sample are public contract summaries, not proof of a deployed hosted export/import service
PUBLIC_EVALUATION_PACKET.json is a reviewer shortcut layer and must stay subordinate to the stronger docs, schemas, release evidence, and machine-readable source surfaces
PUBLIC_FAILURE_MODEL.json is a bounded negative-evidence summary and must stay subordinate to the stronger examples, validator docs, OpenAPI, and portability surfaces
PUBLIC_TRACEABILITY_MATRIX.json is a curated cross-surface map and can drift if maintainers stop updating it together with docs, schemas, examples, and verification artifacts
PUBLIC_EXAMPLE_COVERAGE.json is a curated coverage summary and can drift if maintainers add or remove examples without updating the coverage layer
